title: Do "--cache-from" and "--cache-to" really require "--layers"?
body:
### Issue Description

In the documentation for --cache-from and --cache-to is stated that --layers is required. However:
1. ... I have observed that --layers default value is ```true```
2. ... I have run a couple of tests with and without --layers being set, and the results are the same: caches are reported to be used

without layers:
```
$ podman images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" | grep 'registry.org/lists/builder' | xargs -r podman rmi
$ podman system prune -f
$ time podman build -f docker/lists/ci/Dockerfile -t registry.org/lists/lists:test --cache-from registry.org/lists/builder --cache-to registry.org/lists/builder .
STEP 1/4: FROM registry.org/lists/lists:acc
STEP 2/4: ADD requirements.dev.txt /tmp
--> Cache pulled from remote registry.org/lists/builder:95a4650b539ccea9d93d38c7d1ee6a7e80fa52e3d975097fe7e8af6b1db6053c:95a4650b539ccea9d93d38c7d1ee6a7e80fa52e3d975097fe7e8af6b1db6053c
--> Using cache 5a0e4396325023047038d3d49e7a68a03d35d33bb989136307b63bc148c4b965
--> 5a0e43963250
STEP 3/4: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache     apk add --no-cache --virtual devreqs gcc python3-dev musl-dev linux-headers &&     pip3 install -t /app/python_modules_test -r /tmp/requirements.dev.txt --no-compile &&     apk del devreqs
--> Cache pulled from remote registry.org/lists/builder:72229fe5ee2420647a548eb64a9cca511875b4c9f793fb990adbe7facaa1627f:72229fe5ee2420647a548eb64a9cca511875b4c9f793fb990adbe7facaa1627f
--> Using cache 265709f9d4506055aa03577e6bc0791ba2be165871259f39bb549ef3c1a5d6f2
--> 265709f9d450
STEP 4/4: ENV PYTHONPATH="${PYTHONPATH}:/app/python_modules_test" PATH="${PATH}:/app/python_modules_test/bin"
--> Using cache 76e6d4f2beace6f341d344243be60d64c273bfdaf66860b82eb3f6fa00ced83c
COMMIT registry.org/lists/lists:test
--> Pushing cache [registry.org/lists/builder]:894d3a64c54930b8cf90f268c16bf42684c57be17241fb1ad074487a8e867727
--> 76e6d4f2beac
Successfully tagged registry.org/lists/lists:test
76e6d4f2beace6f341d344243be60d64c273bfdaf66860b82eb3f6fa00ced83c

real    0m1.775s
user    0m0.376s
sys     0m0.280s
```
with ```--layers```
```
$ podman images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" | grep 'registry.org/lists/builder' | xargs -r podman rmi
$ podman system prune -f
$ time podman build -f docker/lists/ci/Dockerfile -t registry.org/lists/lists:test --cache-from registry.org/lists/builder --cache-to registry.org/lists/builder --layers .
STEP 1/4: FROM registry.org/lists/lists:acc
STEP 2/4: ADD requirements.dev.txt /tmp
--> Cache pulled from remote registry.org/lists/builder:95a4650b539ccea9d93d38c7d1ee6a7e80fa52e3d975097fe7e8af6b1db6053c:95a4650b539ccea9d93d38c7d1ee6a7e80fa52e3d975097fe7e8af6b1db6053c
--> Using cache 5a0e4396325023047038d3d49e7a68a03d35d33bb989136307b63bc148c4b965
--> 5a0e43963250
STEP 3/4: RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache     apk add --no-cache --virtual devreqs gcc python3-dev musl-dev linux-headers &&     pip3 install -t /app/python_modules_test -r /tmp/requirements.dev.txt --no-compile &&     apk del devreqs
--> Cache pulled from remote registry.org/lists/builder:72229fe5ee2420647a548eb64a9cca511875b4c9f793fb990adbe7facaa1627f:72229fe5ee2420647a548eb64a9cca511875b4c9f793fb990adbe7facaa1627f
--> Using cache 265709f9d4506055aa03577e6bc0791ba2be165871259f39bb549ef3c1a5d6f2
--> 265709f9d450
STEP 4/4: ENV PYTHONPATH="${PYTHONPATH}:/app/python_modules_test" PATH="${PATH}:/app/python_modules_test/bin"
--> Using cache 76e6d4f2beace6f341d344243be60d64c273bfdaf66860b82eb3f6fa00ced83c
COMMIT registry.org/lists/lists:test
--> Pushing cache [registry.org/lists/builder]:894d3a64c54930b8cf90f268c16bf42684c57be17241fb1ad074487a8e867727
--> 76e6d4f2beac
Successfully tagged registry.org/lists/lists:test
76e6d4f2beace6f341d344243be60d64c273bfdaf66860b82eb3f6fa00ced83c

real    0m1.828s
user    0m0.395s
sys     0m0.231s
```
if cache is not used (so no --cache-from, --cache-to, or --layers flags are set), the build takes 1 minute.

Is this a documentation error?



### Steps to reproduce the issue

There is no issue, is a documentation question.

### Describe the results you received

There is no issue, is a documentation question.

### Describe the results you expected

There is no issue, is a documentation question.

### Additional information

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hi @flixman ,

The documentation is inherited from buildah where `--layers` is not set by default, the documentation intends to say that `--layers` must be true and in `podman` `--layers=true` is set by default so `--cache-to` and `--cache-from` works correctly in `podman` without the flag.

I hope that clears your concern, the documentation is correct in my opinion for the users who expect these flags to work with `--layers=false`.
